channel.title: Artificial intelligence

item.title: Can OpenAI’s GPT-5 model live up to sky-high expectations?
item.description: Release of eagerly awaited system upgrade has been met with a mixed response, with some users calling the gains ‘modest’

item.title: The fund manager of the future might just be a machine
item.description: What if AI doesn’t just enhance active investing, but renders much of it obsolete?

item.title: AI is coming for (some) finance jobs
item.description: The coming quant crunch?

item.title: Could AI help America out of its debt hole?
item.description: Some investors believe the technology’s productivity benefits will put the US on a better fiscal path

item.title: HMRC must disclose whether it used AI in tax credit decisions, court rules
item.description: Revenue had rejected transparency request from tax adviser who believed it used technology to reject R&D applications

item.title: Axes of Evil: ChartCrimeGPT
item.description: Y oh Y

item.title: OpenAI unveils long-awaited GPT-5 model
item.description: Chief Sam Altman hailed next-generation system as ‘major upgrade’

item.title: The risk of putting the ‘open’ back into OpenAI
item.description: Following China’s success, US companies are hoping to defend the centrality of their commercial models

item.title: Online learning stocks deserve a better grade
item.description: Education apps should be a beneficiary of AI, retooling some workers and helping others to keep jobs they already have

item.title: How more efficient data centres could unlock the AI boom
item.description: Efficiency is becoming a bigger priority as tech companies confront strained energy resources

item.title: SoftBank’s $2.9bn profits buoyed by Nvidia investment
item.description: Masayoshi Son’s technology group has been making aggressive AI investments

item.title: Musk to introduce ads to X’s AI chatbot
item.description: Billionaire owner outlines plans to improve advertising on social media platform in rare overture to marketers

item.title: The AI race
item.description: A three-part series exploring the unrelenting quest for AI capacity and the data centres at the heart of hundreds of billions of dollars in capital investment

item.title: Apple hit by string of departures in AI talent war
item.description: Tech group has lost about a dozen of its AI staff to rivals since start of year

item.title: Can energy-hungry data centres be green?
item.description: Energy demand for training machines and running apps is driving a surge of investment into fossil fuels

item.title: How can businesses harness the benefits of older workers?
item.description: Having different generations working together can help them bring out the best in each other

item.title: OpenAI-backed Chai raises $70mn for AI-driven drug discovery
item.description: Start-up has attracted investment from Menlo Ventures and Yuri Milner’s DST Global Partners

item.title: How to reason with OpenAI’s spiralling valuation
item.description: Whether using traditional methods or a more creative approach, investors are backing Sam Altman’s company

item.title: Brookfield steps up bet on AI mania
item.description: Asset management firm says technology is ‘defining’ investment theme of the decade

item.title: Big Four face AI competition from smaller firms, says former EY UK boss
item.description: Hywel Ball says consulting firms’ own AI efforts are ‘doubly important’ as he takes up board roles at two start-ups

item.title: OpenAI in talks for share sale valuing ChatGPT maker at $500bn
item.description: Secondary deal would make the company world’s most valuable private technology group

item.title: OpenAI releases open models to compete with China’s DeepSeek
item.description: Launch of free and more transparent AI systems marks departure from group’s subscription-based closed approach

item.title: Trump official urges Asia to reject Europe’s ‘over-regulation’ of AI
item.description: White House technology adviser Michael Kratsios calls on Asian countries to adopt US artificial intelligence systems

item.title: CoreWeave’s $9bn takeover of Core Scientific faces revolt from top shareholders
item.description: Some investment funds may vote against deal unless protections are added to limit the potential downside

item.title: Palantir’s valuation is a triumph of ‘number go up’
item.description: The data analytics group is an example of how it’s possible to be a successful company at a nonsensical price
